summary: Benchmark scope is the macro loop: multi-sprint, multi-release coordination for sustained software engineering
context: Second attempt at superseding 01KQ0NKTPS88P9Q0JFBT5K8EC9 after the NL parser in twining_record truncated both summary and rationale and detected only 1 of 4 alternatives. Written with structured fields directly in the decision JSON.
rationale: No existing benchmark measures sustained-codebase coordination across sprints and releases. LongMemEval/LoCoMo measure long-conversation retrieval. tau-bench/CooperBench measure tool-use and task completion. REP measures non-coding coordination. Memory-framework comparisons report retrieval scores on conversational data, not task outcomes on sustained codebases. Agent Teams (Anthropic, Feb 2026) operates inside the inner loop and is reframed as orthogonal to substrate choice. Current 892-session results are pilot evidence; the contribution is the evaluation methodology for a category that does not currently exist.
constraints: Must distinguish from Anthropic Agent Teams positioning
Must frame around production-quality sustained software rather than greenfield POCs
Must preserve current pilot data as supporting evidence without overclaiming

alternatives.reason_rejected: Demoted to Phase 1 supporting result; used as a context-reconstitution proxy, not the headline claim.
Absorbed into the broader macro-loop scorecard as one finding among several.

alternatives.option: Minimal coordination budget / lite-matches-full as headline
alternatives.reason_rejected: Weakened by current sprint-sim data at n=16 (d=2.12 full vs 1.19 lite); not strong enough to anchor on.
Wrong layer — Agent Teams is intra-loop parallelism, not sprint-over-sprint coordination. It belongs inside the inner loop as orthogonal tooling.

assumptions: Anthropic Agent Teams (Feb 2026) targets the inner coordination loop, not cross-sprint sustained-codebase evolution
Existing benchmarks (LongMemEval, LoCoMo, tau-bench, CooperBench, REP) do not measure multi-sprint/multi-release coordination on a sustained codebase
892-session pilot is directional, not confirmatory — sample sizes and scenario mix were chosen for single-sprint signal, not macro-loop validation
